text.heading: The Apprentice Africa: Home goes Kathleen
text.p: This season’s The Apprentice Africa edged one episode closer to the end yesterday with the sack of Kathleen. She is the thirteenth apprentice wannabe to be shown the door out of the contest.

In this episode, the two teams, now equal in manpower with three persons apiece were asked to create an interactive sales event in order to a sell a repackaged brand of Tetmosol to its prime distributors in the country. Tetmosol is one of the major products of Jagal Pharma Limited, a subsidiary of Jagal Group and also a co-sponsor of The Apprentice Africa. The team with the higher profit margin at the end of the task wins.

Isaac had earlier been chosen as Project Manager for the Matrix corps while Deox was chosen as PM for Zulu corps. Interestingly, the two Project Managers had something in common; they had both been project managers on two occasions each. More interesting is the fact that on those two occasions, their teams went on to win the task. Hence, one persons winning streak as Project Manager was to end in last night’s episode.
According to the CEO{Biodun Shobanjo}, this week’s task is aimed at bringing to the fore the persuasive ability of both teams - in his exact words,'the art of persuasion'.

The execution of the task began with a brief session with the CEO of Jagal Group, Mr. Hargrav Richard, to orientate both teams on the rudiments of the trade. Both teams also got a PA each to assist them in the execution of the task.

Both teams then went about the execution of thier tasks in the manner they both know best. Matrix corps seemed to be at a disadvantage when Kathleen could not control her anger while negotiating with one of their prospective customers.

There was also a little hitch when Blessing of Zulu corps promised one of the distributors that the incentives being given for purchases were on for six months whilst they were actually meant to last two months only.
The main action in this tasks for the contestants was to sell more products whilst offering less incentives to the distributors.
At the boardroom, both team leaders of the corps were optimistic of their teams' success underlining the fact that they all gave their best.

Before delivering the verdict, Richard noted that Zulu corps was much more confident in executing their task and they closed their deals sooner than their Matrix counterparts. He further noted that Matrix corp was weaker and less organised than Zulu and that they got desperate at a point in the execution of the task.
The verdict. After summing up the sales of both teams and the incentives they offered, Matrix corps had a profit of $160,000 while Zulu corps made a total profit of $192,000. Zulu corp won.

Asked what went wrong, Isaac of Matrix corps, without mincing words, pointed accusing fingers at Kathleen. He went on to give an analysis of her behaviour since the contest began saying she had poor interpersonal relations which cost them a lot in the task. He concluded by sayng if Kathleen had given him half of what Eunice gave him, it would have been a different story.
Eunice supported his views and for that reason, Kathleen was FIRED!!!
This leaves just five contestants in the race for africa's prime apprentice.
